news_summary: Scientists have discovered a hard yet lightweight bio-metal material in sea worm jaws, promising advancements in durable and efficient technology materials.
news_summary_fr: Des scientifiques ont découvert dans les mâchoires d'un ver marin un matériau biométallique à la fois résistant et léger, qui laisse entrevoir des avancées prometteuses dans le domaine des matériaux technologiques durables et performants.
news_summary_es: Los científicos han descubierto un material biometálico, duro pero ligero, en las mandíbulas de un gusano marino, lo que abre nuevas perspectivas para el desarrollo de materiales tecnológicos duraderos y eficientes.

articles.title: Tech Life
articles.summary: Surgeons remotely control two human-like robots in an operating theatre.
articles.content:
Available for over a year

Humanoid robots have successfully performed surgery in an operating theatre for the first time. They were remotely controlled by surgeons. An expert involved talks to Tech Life. Also this week: the man who invented the infinite scroll tells us his latest thoughts on social media. And how do you stop big cargo ships pumping greenhouse gases into the air, as they move our goods around the world? We hear about a carbon capture solution. Presenter: Shiona McCallum Producer: Tom Quinn (Photo: Two human-shaped robots stand holding equipment, and perform surgery in an operating theatre. Credit: University of California San Diego)

Programme Website
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant technological breakthrough where humanoid robots have successfully performed surgery remotely, demonstrating a real-world impact with potential broad benefits for healthcare. The story is focused on this achievement and provides enough context to understand its significance.
articles.personality_title: Surgeons control humanoid robots to perform remote surgery
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Surgeons usually operate directly on patients in hospitals. Robots have helped in surgery before, but this time, human-like robots were controlled from a distance to perform real surgery.

**What happened** – For the first time, surgeons remotely controlled two humanoid robots during an actual operation in an operating theatre. These robots look like humans and were able to hold tools and perform surgical tasks. The event took place recently and was reported by the University of California San Diego.

**Impact** – This is important because it shows robots can help surgeons work from far away, which might make surgery possible in places without expert doctors nearby. It could help patients in remote areas get better care and reduce travel for doctors.

**What's next step** – Researchers will likely test this technology more to make sure it is safe and reliable. They may also work on improving the robots’ abilities and exploring ways to use them in different types of surgeries.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Surgeons have successfully used remote-controlled humanoid robots to perform surgery, opening new possibilities for medical care in distant locations.
articles.personality_title_fr: Des chirurgiens contrôlent à distance des robots humanoïdes pour une chirurgie réussie
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Habituellement, les chirurgiens opèrent directement sur les patients dans les hôpitaux. Les robots ont déjà aidé en chirurgie, mais cette fois, des robots ressemblant à des humains ont été contrôlés à distance pour réaliser une vraie opération.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Pour la première fois, des chirurgiens ont contrôlé à distance deux robots humanoïdes pendant une opération dans une salle d’opération. Ces robots ont pu tenir des outils et effectuer des gestes chirurgicaux. Cette avancée a été rapportée par l’Université de Californie à San Diego.

**Impact** – C’est important car cela montre que les robots peuvent aider les chirurgiens à travailler de loin, ce qui pourrait permettre des opérations dans des endroits sans médecins spécialistes. Cela pourrait améliorer les soins pour les patients éloignés et éviter aux médecins de se déplacer.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs vont probablement tester cette technologie davantage pour assurer sa sécurité et sa fiabilité. Ils chercheront aussi à améliorer les capacités des robots et à les utiliser dans différents types d’opérations.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Des chirurgiens ont utilisé avec succès des robots humanoïdes contrôlés à distance pour réaliser une chirurgie, ouvrant de nouvelles possibilités pour les soins médicaux à distance.
articles.personality_title_es: Cirujanos controlan robots humanoides para realizar cirugía a distancia
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Normalmente, los cirujanos operan directamente a los pacientes en los hospitales. Los robots han ayudado en cirugías antes, pero esta vez, robots con forma humana fueron controlados a distancia para realizar una operación real.

**Qué pasó** – Por primera vez, cirujanos controlaron remotamente dos robots humanoides durante una operación en un quirófano. Estos robots pudieron sostener herramientas y realizar tareas quirúrgicas. El evento fue reportado por la Universidad de California en San Diego.

**Impacto** – Esto es importante porque muestra que los robots pueden ayudar a los cirujanos a trabajar desde lejos, lo que podría hacer posible la cirugía en lugares sin doctores expertos cerca. Podría ayudar a pacientes en áreas remotas a recibir mejor atención y reducir los viajes de los médicos.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores probablemente probarán más esta tecnología para asegurar que sea segura y confiable. También buscarán mejorar las habilidades de los robots y explorar su uso en diferentes tipos de cirugías.

**Resumen en una frase** – Cirujanos han utilizado con éxito robots humanoides controlados a distancia para realizar cirugía, abriendo nuevas posibilidades para la atención médica en lugares lejanos.
articles.image_prompt: Two humanoid robots with sleek, articulated limbs gently holding surgical instruments over an operating table inside a softly lit, modern operating theatre, rendered in warm, natural tones with a painterly style that emphasizes careful precision and advanced technology without showing human faces or detailed anatomy.

articles.title: Hard but lightweight ‘bio-metal’ material discovered in sea worm jaws
articles.summary: Marine bristle worms have jaws made from a mix of proteins and metal ions that may constitute a whole new kind of material, with possible applications in engineering
articles.content:
Marine bristle worms have jaws made from a mix of proteins and metal ions that may constitute a whole new kind of material, with possible applications in engineering

The marine ragworm Perinereis cultrifera Steve Trewhella / Alamy

The jaws of some sea worms are made of an exceptionally hard yet lightweight material dubbed a “bio-metal” that could have applications in engineering.

Perinereis cultrifera is a type of ragworm with a long body adorned with bristles. Members of the species also have strong jaws that enable them to crush hard prey such as small crustaceans or other worms. Remnants of their jaws have been found in the fossil record dating back to hundreds of millions of years ago.

Christian Hellmich at TU Wien in Austria and his colleagues have been studying this worm’s jaws for almost a decade, leading them to propose that they are made of a novel material. The molecular structure of each jaw combines proteins and ions of metals such as zinc, giving it characteristics in between those of softer biological materials and metals.

Advertisement

Most recently, the team performed more than 3300 experiments in which small indentations were made in different parts of the jaw. The way its hardness changed under this pressure followed a pattern typical of metals like copper and silver. But the jaw also exhibited a kind of elasticity that metals cannot have, says Hellmich.

Finally, the researchers developed a mathematical model of bio-metals, which shows how they might respond to strain in a unique way in which microscopic forces arise from the metal ions becoming arranged into lines similar to certain defects in crystals.

Free newsletter Sign up to The Earth Edition Sign up to newsletter

The researchers were surprised to uncover so much novelty in the relatively simple animal. Performing mechanical tests on the millimetre-sized jaw was really challenging and required hundreds of hours of preparation and polishing, says Hellmich. “Basically, anything can go wrong,” he says.

“The jaws of bristle worms are incredibly hard yet very lightweight,” says Matthew Lehnert at Kent State University in Ohio. “Many industries, from automobiles to aeronautics, are searching for new ways to develop hard and lightweight materials. The answers are provided in nature!”

“Somehow evolution figured out a way to coax a metal-like mechanical fingerprint out of protein-like ingredients, and studying the worm is how we ask what trick makes that possible,” says Markus Buehler at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, who didn’t work on the study. The long-term dream outcome of this research is to genetically program materials that would grow in biological systems, he says.

Hellmich and his colleagues are interested in pursuing this goal and their team already includes geneticists and biologists at the University of Vienna. “We are asking questions like, ‘If we knock out a few genes, then how will the jaws be different?’” he says.
articles.reasoning: The discovery of a novel bio-metal material in sea worm jaws represents a significant scientific breakthrough with potential broad applications in engineering and materials science, offering a new approach to creating hard yet lightweight materials inspired by nature. The article provides detailed context on the research, its novelty, and future implications, indicating meaningful real-world impact beyond a niche audience.
articles.personality_title: Scientists discover new lightweight ‘bio-metal’ in sea worm jaws
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Some sea worms, called ragworms, have very strong jaws that help them crush hard prey like small crabs. These jaws have been around for hundreds of millions of years and are made of a special mix of proteins and metals.

**What happened** – Researchers in Austria studied the jaws of the ragworm species Perinereis cultrifera for nearly ten years. They found that the jaws are made of a new kind of material combining proteins and metal ions like zinc. This material is very hard like metal but also light and flexible, which is unusual. They tested the jaws with over 3,300 small pressure experiments and created a mathematical model to explain how this “bio-metal” works.

**Impact** – This discovery is important because it reveals a material that is both strong and lightweight, qualities that many industries like car or airplane manufacturing want. The material’s unique mix of metal-like hardness and biological flexibility could inspire new ways to make tough, light materials.

**What's next step** – The scientists plan to study the genetics behind how these jaws form. By understanding which genes control the bio-metal, they hope to learn how to create similar materials in the lab or even grow them inside living things.

**One-sentence takeaway** – Scientists have found a new, strong yet light material in sea worm jaws that could lead to better engineering materials inspired by nature.
articles.personality_title_fr: Des scientifiques découvrent un nouveau « bio-métal » léger dans les mâchoires de vers marins
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Certains vers marins, appelés vers à soies, ont des mâchoires très solides qui leur permettent de broyer des proies dures comme de petits crabes. Ces mâchoires existent depuis des centaines de millions d’années et sont faites d’un mélange spécial de protéines et de métaux.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Des chercheurs en Autriche ont étudié pendant près de dix ans les mâchoires du ver Perinereis cultrifera. Ils ont découvert que ces mâchoires sont composées d’un nouveau type de matériau combinant des protéines et des ions métalliques comme le zinc. Ce matériau est très dur comme un métal, mais aussi léger et flexible, ce qui est inhabituel. Ils ont réalisé plus de 3 300 expériences de pression sur les mâchoires et créé un modèle mathématique pour expliquer ce « bio-métal ».

**Impact** – Cette découverte est importante car elle révèle un matériau à la fois solide et léger, des qualités recherchées par de nombreuses industries comme l’automobile ou l’aéronautique. Le mélange unique de dureté métallique et de flexibilité biologique pourrait inspirer de nouvelles façons de fabriquer des matériaux résistants et légers.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques veulent étudier la génétique qui contrôle la formation de ces mâchoires. En comprenant quels gènes dirigent le bio-métal, ils espèrent apprendre à créer des matériaux similaires en laboratoire ou même à les faire pousser dans des organismes vivants.

**Phrase clé** – Des scientifiques ont découvert un nouveau matériau solide mais léger dans les mâchoires de vers marins, qui pourrait conduire à de meilleurs matériaux d’ingénierie inspirés par la nature.
articles.personality_title_es: Científicos descubren un nuevo ‘bio-metal’ ligero en las mandíbulas de gusanos marinos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Algunos gusanos marinos, llamados gusanos con cerdas, tienen mandíbulas muy fuertes que les permiten triturar presas duras como pequeños cangrejos. Estas mandíbulas existen desde hace cientos de millones de años y están hechas de una mezcla especial de proteínas y metales.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores en Austria estudiaron durante casi diez años las mandíbulas del gusano Perinereis cultrifera. Descubrieron que están hechas de un nuevo tipo de material que combina proteínas e iones metálicos como el zinc. Este material es muy duro como el metal pero también ligero y flexible, algo poco común. Realizaron más de 3,300 pruebas de presión pequeñas y crearon un modelo matemático para explicar cómo funciona este “bio-metal”.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento es importante porque muestra un material que es fuerte y ligero, cualidades que muchas industrias como la automotriz y la aeronáutica buscan. La mezcla única de dureza metálica y flexibilidad biológica podría inspirar nuevas maneras de fabricar materiales resistentes y livianos.

**Próximo paso** – Los científicos planean estudiar la genética que controla la formación de estas mandíbulas. Entendiendo qué genes regulan el bio-metal, esperan aprender a crear materiales similares en el laboratorio o incluso cultivarlos dentro de organismos vivos.

**Resumen en una frase** – Científicos han encontrado un nuevo material fuerte pero ligero en las mandíbulas de gusanos marinos que podría llevar a mejores materiales de ingeniería inspirados en la naturaleza.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a segmented marine ragworm with textured bristles, its translucent jaws glowing softly with intertwined strands of protein fibers and shimmering metallic ions like zinc, set against a simple oceanic background that highlights the fusion of natural organic forms and crystalline metal-like structures.
